title: [HOLD App/pull/80209] [$250] Refactor `getPolicyExpenseReportOption()` to change `privateIsArchived` parameter to boolean

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
coming from https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/80209/changes#diff-010911fd56cd74ff46fd42cd2a137e01f8a050eaf4a038d837ffa3b5912a5607 cc @dukenv0307 # Problem There is a long chain of code that starts with `getPolicyExpenseReportOption()` accepting a string value for `privateIsArchived`. When you trace the code down through `createOption()`, you can see that `privateIsArchived` is always cast to a boolean by prepending the value with `!!`. # Solution Update the code path for this value to be a boolean all the way from the view components (use the `useReportIsArchived()` hook) down to where the value is used.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @QichenZhu

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.33-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-7802/folder/13176691/41236748/1233304337 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** applausetester+ck3726@applause.expensifail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Windows 11/Chrome, iPhone 16/iOS 18.7.1 **App Component:** Workspace Settings ## Action Performed: 1. Navigate to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Log in with new expensifail account (that does not have any bank account already added) 3. Create a new workspace 4. Set its primary currency to "AUD" 5. Navigate to Workspace settings > Workflows > Add bank account > Connect manually 6. Proceed through the bank account connection flow until you reach Step 3: Business Information (Enter random details for all the fields for step 1 and 2 which does not trigger error) 7. Enter random business name and website 8. Enter 24 302 976 253 as business registration number >> Click Next 9. Enter 24 302 976 253 as "ABN" >> Click Next 10. Click outside the RHP to close or exit the "Connect Bank Account" flow 11. Navigate to "Workspace Overview" 12. Update the currency either to "CAD", "EUR" or "GBP" ## Expected Result: User able to update currency in overview when WS has partially added non-US bank account ## Actual Result: Unable to update currency in overview when WS has partially added non-US bank account ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/16fcb70d-3e5b-4fa1-b661-691a1227dd50 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
Similar to [functionality and logic](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/81803) recently added for approval workflows, let's add a quick search filter for merchant rules. **Design and product details:** - The quick filter appears when a user more than 3 distinct merchant rules - The search input search the merchant corresponding tot to the merchant rule - We'll use "contains" logic for the search query

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.33-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Unable to validate in Prod - Provide reasoning. **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/folder/13176677/test-cases/41237668 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** testexpensify9+la1333zxc@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Windows 11, Chrome **App Component:** User Settings ## Action Performed: Preconditions: - Go to https://staging.new.expensify.com/, press F12 (open Developer tools) > Application > Cookies > https://staging.new.expensify.com/ Add the following cookies: lc-qa = 2 lc-debug = 1 1. Go to https://staging.new.expensify.com/ 2. Sign up with a Gmail address with a + 3. Choose "Manage my team's expenses" and select QBD (complete the other options with any selection) 4. Open console and enter the following snippet (edit to be the actual date): Onyx.merge('nvp_private_lastDayFreeTrial', '2026-3-7 01:01:00'); 5. Run the following snippet: Onyx.merge('nvp_private_amountOwed', 1000); 6. Run the following snippet: Onyx.merge('nvp_private_billingGracePeriodEnd', 1719792000); 7. Click FAB > Create manual expense > Enter any amount > Select the Workspace ## Expected Result: When creating an expense with ended free trial, the payment paywall should be displayed. ## Actual Result: When creating an expense, the payment paywall appears and disappears briefly. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fb90d31c-69b0-4618-9545-f79eb45e48f8 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
